data: Revise how to write professional emails for interviews

message: Let's revise how to write professional emails for interview-related communication.
image_url:
button_text: Continue!

message: See this hint image to refresh your memory. ☝️
image_url:
button_text: Next!

message: Great! Now answer some questions using the hint image.
image_url:
button_text: Start Practice!

Select the correct professional email opening line

1.I am writing to express my interest in the position and I look forward to discussing my application further.
2.I want the job and I will be great at it, please respond soon.

Correct answer: 1

Select the correct way to write a follow-up email

1.You haven't replied so I am emailing again to remind you.
2.I am writing to follow up on my interview and I look forward to receiving your decision.

Correct answer: 2
I am writing to confirm my attendance at the scheduled interview and I look forward to the discussion.
I am writing to introduce myself as a candidate for the open position and I look forward to your consideration.
I am writing to request a rescheduling of the interview and I look forward to your understanding.
I am writing to express gratitude for the opportunity given and I look forward to contributing to the team.
I am writing to submit my project report as requested and I look forward to your valuable feedback.

first_message: That was incredible! Now we're going to practice by answering few questions in English. Can we start?
prompt_id: 4d3f5213-fcc4-40f3-879d-051908377a31
questions: "'1. You want to confirm an interview appointment. Write the opening line of your email.'

'2. You want to introduce yourself for a job opening. Write the opening line of your professional email.'

'3. You want to request a rescheduling of a meeting. Write the opening line of your email.'

'4. You want to thank the recruiter after an interview. Write the opening line of your email.'

'5. You want to follow up on a job offer. Write the opening line of your professional email.'"

sentence_structure: "I am writing to [purpose] and I look forward to [response]."
